sanskrit: अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् । देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु उन अल्पबुद्धिवाले मनुष्योंको उन देवताओंकी आराधनाका फल अन्तवाला नाशवान् ही मिलता है ।  देवताओंका पूजन करनेवाले देवताओंको प्राप्त होते हैं और मेरे भक्त मेरे ही प्राप्त होते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: But the reward gained by those of limited understanding is finite. Those who worship the radiant beings go to the radiant beings; my devotees come to me.
essence: Finite worship brings finite results; devotion returns you to Krishna.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Half-hearted understanding gives limited results. Those who worship lesser powers reach those powers, while Krishna's devotees reach Krishna.
simpleInsight: Your deepest allegiance determines your destination.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is still frozen while Krishna explains devotion. After showing how faith can be placed in many forms, Krishna draws the line: limited worship gives limited fruit, but devotion to him leads to him.
modernRelevance: You keep checking three different opinions before sending the message that matters. The mind wants a safe outcome, but the heart follows the thing it truly serves.
translationLiteral: अन्तवत्तु (finite indeed) / फलम् (fruit, result) / तेषाम् (of those) / तत् (that) / भवति (becomes) / अल्पमेधसाम् (of the little-wise) / देवान् (to the radiant beings) / देवयजः (worshippers of the radiant beings) / यान्ति (go) / मद्भक्ताः (my devotees) / यान्ति (go) / माम् (to me) / अपि (also)
